message:
feat(cancellation_safe_channel): Cx-first reserve + recv (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Add Cx-first siblings to the two public async methods on the
cancellation-safe transactional channel. This channel is
specifically designed for no-silent-loss semantics — making
its waiters cancellation-aware aligns well with its existing
reserve/commit contract.

Additions (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `TxProducer::reserve_with_cx(cx)` — Cx-first parallel of
    `reserve`. Checkpoint at loop top breaks a caller out of a
    blocked `capacity_freed.notified().await`. Returns
    `Err(Closed)` on pre-cancel or mid-wait cancel — matches
    the close-while-waiting return shape so existing callers
    that handle `Closed` need no changes.

  * `TxConsumer::recv_with_cx(cx)` — Cx-first parallel of
    `recv`. Returns `None` on pre-cancel or mid-wait cancel —
    matches the closed-and-drained return shape.

Both stay faithful to the module's cancellation-safety theme:
a cancelled reserve leaves no partial state (no reservation
was ever issued), and a cancelled recv leaves the queue
untouched. The module already guarantees value-level
cancellation-safety via the reserve+commit token; these cx
additions extend that guarantee to the wait-loop level.

Regression test (new) `tx_channel_with_cx_round_trip`:

  * Opens a channel with capacity=4.
  * Reserves via `reserve_with_cx` → asserts seq=1,
    active_reservations=1.
  * Commits "cx-hello" via the sync `commit` method — proves
    the sync path interoperates with the Cx-first reserve.
  * Receives via `recv_with_cx` → asserts value + seq match.

Uses raw `runtime.block_on` harness (matches ticks 77/83
pattern) because cancellation_safe_channel::tests uses sync
fixtures via std::thread::Barrier.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * cancellation_safe_channel:: — 23 passed / 0 failed (tick
    84: 22 -> tick 85: 23, +1 new round-trip parity test)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (30.82s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Cx-first cancellation_safe_channel.rs trail COMPLETE — both
public async methods have cx siblings.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
